verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Job, dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: And Job answered and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Ergo vos estis soli homines,  et vobiscum morietur sapientia ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אׇ֭מְנָם כִּ֣י אַתֶּם־עָ֑ם וְ֝עִמָּכֶ֗ם תָּמ֥וּת חׇכְמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: No doubt but ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mihi est cor sicut et vobis, nec inferior vestri sum ;  quis enim hæc quæ nostis ignorat ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־לִ֤י לֵבָ֨ב ׀ כְּֽמוֹכֶ֗ם לֹא־נֹפֵ֣ל אָנֹכִ֣י מִכֶּ֑ם וְאֶת־מִי־אֵ֥ין כְּמוֹ־אֵֽלֶּה׃
verses.king_james: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you: yea, who knoweth not such things as these?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui deridetur ab amico suo, sicut ego,  invocabit Deum, et exaudiet eum :  deridetur enim justi simplicitas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂחֹ֤ק לְרֵעֵ֨הוּ ׀ אֶהְיֶ֗ה קֹרֵ֣א לֶ֭אֱלוֹהַּ וַֽיַּעֲנֵ֑הוּ שְׂ֝ח֗וֹק צַדִּ֥יק תָּמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: I am as one mocked of his neighbour, who calleth upon God, and he answereth him: the just upright man is laughed to scorn.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lampas contempta apud cogitationes divitum  parata ad tempus statutum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַפִּ֣יד בּ֭וּז לְעַשְׁתּ֣וּת שַׁאֲנָ֑ן נָ֝כ֗וֹן לְמ֣וֹעֲדֵי רָֽגֶל׃
verses.king_james: He that is ready to slip with his feet is as a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abundant tabernacula prædonum,  et audacter provocant Deum,  cum ipse dederit omnia in manus eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִשְׁלָ֤יוּ אֹהָלִ֨ים ׀ לְשֹׁ֥דְדִ֗ים וּֽ֭בַטֻּחוֹת לְמַרְגִּ֣יזֵי אֵ֑ל לַאֲשֶׁ֤ר הֵבִ֖יא אֱל֣וֹהַּ בְּיָדֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The tabernacles of robbers prosper, and they that provoke God are secure; into whose hand God bringeth abundantly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nimirum interroga jumenta, et docebunt te ;  et volatilia cæli, et indicabunt tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽאוּלָ֗ם שְׁאַל־נָ֣א בְהֵמ֣וֹת וְתֹרֶ֑ךָּ וְע֥וֹף הַ֝שָּׁמַ֗יִם וְיַגֶּד־לָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: But ask now the beasts, and they shall teach thee; and the fowls of the air, and they shall tell thee:

verses.latin_vulgate: Loquere terræ, et respondebit tibi,  et narrabunt pisces maris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: א֤וֹ שִׂ֣יחַ לָאָ֣רֶץ וְתֹרֶ֑ךָּ וִיסַפְּר֥וּ לְ֝ךָ֗ דְּגֵ֣י הַיָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach thee: and the fishes of the sea shall declare unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis ignorat quod omnia hæc manus Domini fecerit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֭י לֹא־יָדַ֣ע בְּכׇל־אֵ֑לֶּה כִּ֥י יַד־יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה עָ֣שְׂתָה זֹּֽאת׃
verses.king_james: Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the Lord hath wrought this?

verses.latin_vulgate: In cujus manu anima omnis viventis,  et spiritus universæ carnis hominis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּ֭יָדוֹ נֶ֣פֶשׁ כׇּל־חָ֑י וְ֝ר֗וּחַ כׇּל־בְּשַׂר־אִֽישׁ׃
verses.king_james: In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne auris verba dijudicat ?  et fauces comedentis, saporem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹא־אֹ֭זֶן מִלִּ֣ין תִּבְחָ֑ן וְ֝חֵ֗ךְ אֹ֣כֶל יִטְעַם־לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Doth not the ear try words? and the mouth taste his meat?

verses.latin_vulgate: In antiquis est sapientia,  et in multo tempore prudentia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּישִׁישִׁ֥ים חׇכְמָ֑ה וְאֹ֖רֶךְ יָמִ֣ים תְּבוּנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: With the ancient is wisdom; and in length of days understanding.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apud ipsum est sapientia et fortitudo ;  ipse habet consilium et intelligentiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִ֭מּוֹ חׇכְמָ֣ה וּגְבוּרָ֑ה ל֝֗וֹ עֵצָ֥ה וּתְבוּנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si destruxerit, nemo est qui ædificet ;  si incluserit hominem, nullus est qui aperiat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֣ן יַ֭הֲרוֹס וְלֹ֣א יִבָּנֶ֑ה יִסְגֹּ֥ר עַל־אִ֝֗ישׁ וְלֹ֣א יִפָּתֵֽחַ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, he breaketh down, and it cannot be built again: he shutteth up a man, and there can be no opening.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si continuerit aquas, omnia siccabuntur ;  et si emiserit eas, subvertent terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤ן יַעְצֹ֣ר בַּמַּ֣יִם וְיִבָ֑שׁוּ וִ֝ישַׁלְּחֵ֗ם וְיַ֖הַפְכוּ־אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apud ipsum est fortitudo et sapientia ;  ipse novit et decipientem, et eum qui decipitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִ֭מּוֹ עֹ֣ז וְתוּשִׁיָּ֑ה ל֝֗וֹ שֹׁגֵ֥ג וּמַשְׁגֶּֽה׃
verses.king_james: With him is strength and wisdom: the deceived and the deceiver are his.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adducit consiliarios in stultum finem,  et judices in stuporem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מוֹלִ֣יךְ יוֹעֲצִ֣ים שׁוֹלָ֑ל וְֽשֹׁפְטִ֥ים יְהוֹלֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: He leadeth counsellors away spoiled, and maketh the judges fools.

verses.latin_vulgate: Balteum regum dissolvit,  et præcingit fune renes eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מוּסַ֣ר מְלָכִ֣ים פִּתֵּ֑חַ וַיֶּאְסֹ֥ר אֵ֝ז֗וֹר בְּמׇתְנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: He looseth the bond of kings, and girdeth their loins with a girdle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ducit sacerdotes inglorios,  et optimates supplantat :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מוֹלִ֣יךְ כֹּהֲנִ֣ים שׁוֹלָ֑ל וְאֵ֖תָנִ֣ים יְסַלֵּֽף׃
verses.king_james: He leadeth princes away spoiled, and overthroweth the mighty.

verses.latin_vulgate: commutans labium veracium,  et doctrinam senum auferens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵסִ֣יר שָׂ֭פָה לְנֶאֱמָנִ֑ים וְטַ֖עַם זְקֵנִ֣ים יִקָּֽח׃
verses.king_james: He removeth away the speech of the trusty, and taketh away the understanding of the aged.

verses.latin_vulgate: Effundit despectionem super principes,  eos qui oppressi fuerant relevans.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׁוֹפֵ֣ךְ בּ֭וּז עַל־נְדִיבִ֑ים וּמְזִ֖יחַ אֲפִיקִ֣ים רִפָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: He poureth contempt upon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mighty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui revelat profunda de tenebris,  et producit in lucem umbram mortis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְגַלֶּ֣ה עֲ֭מֻקוֹת מִנִּי־חֹ֑שֶׁךְ וַיֹּצֵ֖א לָא֣וֹר צַלְמָֽוֶת׃
verses.king_james: He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui multiplicat gentes, et perdit eas,  et subversas in integrum restituit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַשְׂגִּ֣יא לַ֭גּוֹיִם וַֽיְאַבְּדֵ֑ם שֹׁטֵ֥חַ לַ֝גּוֹיִ֗ם וַיַּנְחֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: He increaseth the nations, and destroyeth them: he enlargeth the nations, and straiteneth them again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui immutat cor principum populi terræ,  et decipit eos ut frustra incedant per invium :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵסִ֗יר לֵ֭ב רָאשֵׁ֣י עַם־הָאָ֑רֶץ וַ֝יַּתְעֵ֗ם בְּתֹ֣הוּ לֹא־דָֽרֶךְ׃
verses.king_james: He taketh away the heart of the chief of the people of the earth, and causeth them to wander in a wilderness where there is no way.

verses.latin_vulgate: palpabunt quasi in tenebris, et non in luce,  et errare eos faciet quasi ebrios.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְמַֽשְׁשׁוּ־חֹ֥שֶׁךְ וְלֹא־א֑וֹר וַ֝יַּתְעֵ֗ם כַּשִּׁכּֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: They grope in the dark without light, and he maketh them to stagger like a drunken man.
